title: Palak Paneer Spinach Cottage Cheese

seo.title: Authentic Palak Paneer Recipe: Easy Indian Spinach Curry
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of making delicious Palak Paneer at home! This easy Indian vegetarian recipe features fresh spinach and soft paneer cheese. Get the full step-by-step guide.
lowCompetitionKeywords: Palak Paneer with spinach and paneer
seo.h1: Authentic Palak Paneer Recipe (Indian Spinach & Paneer Curry)
h2Headings: Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of creamy green Palak Paneer with cubes of paneer, garnished with cream and cilantro, served with naan.
seo.imageCaption: Learn how to make this vibrant and flavorful Palak Paneer, a classic Indian vegetarian dish perfect for any meal!

description: Palak Paneer is a quintessential Indian vegetarian dish featuring tender spinach puree and succulent cubes of fresh paneer cottage cheese. This vibrant green curry is subtly spiced, offering a delightful balance of earthy flavors and creamy texture. It's a wholesome and satisfying meal, perfect to enjoy with naan or rice.

instructions.title: Blanch Spinach and Prepare Puree
instructions.text: Bring a large pot of water to a rolling boil. Add the washed spinach and blanch for 2-3 minutes until wilted and bright green. Immediately transfer the spinach to an ice bath to stop cooking and retain its vibrant color. Drain well, squeeze out excess water, and blend into a smooth puree with the green chili.

instructions.title: Lightly Fry Paneer
instructions.text: Heat 1 tablespoon of oil or ghee in a non-stick pan over medium heat. Add the paneer cubes and lightly fry until golden brown on all sides. This adds a nice texture and prevents them from breaking apart in the curry. Remove the paneer and set aside on a plate lined with paper towels.

instructions.title: Sauté Aromatics
instructions.text: In the same pan, add the remaining 1 tablespoon of oil or ghee. Once hot, add the cumin seeds and let them splutter for a few seconds. Then, add the finely chopped onion and sauté until translucent and light golden brown, stirring occasionally.

instructions.title: Cook Ginger, Garlic, and Spices
instructions.text: Add the minced ginger and garlic to the pan and sauté for another minute until fragrant. Stir in the turmeric powder and coriander powder, cooking for about 30 seconds until aromatic, ensuring the spices do not burn.

instructions.title: Add Tomato Puree
instructions.text: Pour in the tomato puree and cook the mixture thoroughly. Continue to cook, stirring occasionally, until the oil begins to separate from the masala, indicating that the tomatoes are well cooked. Add salt to taste at this stage.

instructions.title: Combine Spinach and Paneer
instructions.text: Stir in the prepared spinach puree and 1/2 cup of water. Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer and cook for 5-7 minutes, allowing all the flavors to meld together beautifully. Gently add the fried paneer cubes and garam masala. Mix gently to coat the paneer without breaking it and cook for another 2-3 minutes.

instructions.title: Serve Hot
instructions.text: Garnish the Palak Paneer with a swirl of fresh cream if desired, and a sprinkle of fresh cilantro. Serve hot with your favorite Indian bread like naan, roti, or with steamed basmati rice for a complete and satisfying meal.

tips: To get a vibrant green Palak Paneer, blanch the spinach quickly and immediately plunge it into ice-cold water before blending. This shocks the spinach and locks in its color, preventing it from turning dull.

For a richer flavor and slightly firmer paneer, lightly fry the paneer cubes until golden before adding them to the curry. If you prefer softer paneer, you can add them directly without frying or soak them in warm water after frying for a few minutes.

Adjust the spice level by varying the number of green chilies. For a creamier texture and added richness, you can add a tablespoon of cashew paste along with the tomato puree or a dash of fresh cream at the very end before serving.
article: Palak Paneer Spinach Cottage Cheese is a beloved Indian Vegetarian dish enjoyed across Indian. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
